themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, advocating for preferential treatment of citizens over immigrants in welfare, housing, and social services. This theme is particularly prominent in Spain, France, and Italy, where parties like Vox, Rassemblement National, and Lega are framing these policies as essential to addressing domestic resource scarcity and protecting national identity.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox campaigns on 'national priority' in Andalusia, proposing tax cuts and public fee reductions for Spanish families, and criticizing PP and PSOE for neglecting national interests in favor of immigrants.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN emphasizes 'national priority' in social aid, opposing EU loans for campaigns and advocating for French citizens' preferential access to resources.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega pushes for 'national priority' in housing and welfare policies, criticizing left-wing parties for prioritizing immigrants over Italian citizens.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS criticizes EU climate policies for harming Polish farmers and businesses, advocating for national sovereignty in policy-making.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD frames immigration as a threat to German social systems, advocating for policies that prioritize German citizens in welfare and housing.

themes.title: Hungary’s political transition dominates coverage
themes.blurb: The recent election of Péter Magyar as Hungary's new prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule, has become a dominant cross-national theme. This transition is being closely watched by right-wing and far-right media in the U.S., Poland, Italy, and Slovakia, with a focus on its implications for EU relations, anti-corruption efforts, and the broader shift away from populist governance in Central Europe.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Coverage centers on Magyar's swearing-in as PM, his promises of systemic change, anti-corruption reforms, and restoring EU relations after Orbán's era.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights the end of Orbán's rule, framing it as a shift in Hungary's foreign policy and potential realignment with Western allies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media focuses on the implications of Hungary's transition for regional politics, particularly Poland's strained relations with Hungary under Orbán.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian coverage emphasizes the potential impact of Hungary's political shift on EU dynamics and Italy's diplomatic relations with Budapest.

countries.name: 🇸🇰 General (Slovakia)
countries.note: Slovak media analyzes the broader Central European implications of Hungary's transition, particularly for populist movements in the region.

themes.title: Reform UK’s electoral surge reshapes UK politics
themes.blurb: Reform UK's unprecedented gains in the UK's local elections have sent shockwaves through the political landscape, with the party securing control of multiple councils and positioning itself as a major challenger to the Conservative and Labour parties. This theme is resonating across the U.S., Italy, France, and Spain, where right-wing media are analyzing the implications for British politics and the potential rise of Nigel Farage as a future prime minister.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK gains over 1,400 council seats, taking control of key councils like Essex, Sunderland, and Thurrock, and positioning itself as a major political force.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights Reform UK's success as a model for challenging establishment politics, drawing parallels to Trump's populist movement.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian coverage focuses on the potential impact of Reform UK's rise on UK-EU relations and the broader European far-right movement.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French media analyzes Reform UK's electoral success as part of a broader European trend of far-right parties gaining ground.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish coverage emphasizes the parallels between Reform UK's anti-immigration and anti-establishment rhetoric and Vox's own political platform.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and political influence continue to dominate right-wing and far-right media coverage worldwide. This theme is particularly strong in Italy, Poland, Argentina, and the UK, where media outlets are closely following Trump's stance on international conflicts, trade policies, and his potential impact on global right-wing movements.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Coverage focuses on Trump's policies on Iran, trade, and immigration, as well as his potential re-election campaign and influence on global right-wing movements.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian media analyzes Trump's impact on Italy's foreign policy, particularly his stance on NATO, Iran, and trade relations with the EU.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish coverage highlights Trump's potential influence on U.S.-Poland relations, particularly in defense and energy policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: UK media draws parallels between Trump's populist rhetoric and Reform UK's anti-establishment platform, particularly on immigration and trade.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Argentinian coverage focuses on Trump's economic policies as a model for President Milei's libertarian reforms and his approach to international trade.

themes.title: Corruption scandals rock far-right governments
themes.blurb: Corruption scandals involving high-ranking officials in far-right governments are making headlines across multiple countries. This theme is particularly prominent in Argentina, Hungary, Poland, and Spain, where media outlets are scrutinizing allegations of illicit enrichment, embezzlement, and abuse of power, often framing these scandals as evidence of systemic corruption within right-wing administrations.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Coverage focuses on the scandal involving Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni, accused of illicit enrichment and undeclared cryptocurrency transactions.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungarian media highlights corruption allegations against former Orbán allies, with the new Magyar government vowing to investigate past abuses.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish coverage centers on the scandal involving former Justice Minister Zbigniew Ziobro, accused of embezzlement and fleeing to the U.S.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish media scrutinizes Vox's financial transparency, particularly its reliance on EU funds and allegations of misuse for political campaigns.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights corruption allegations against Trump administration officials, including Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's ties to Jeffrey Epstein.
